message:
feat(fcp-sdk): add limits-aware validation helpers and refactor internal limit checking

Add validate_input_with_limits() and validate_output_with_limits() to
the SDK public API, enabling connectors to enforce payload size, array
length, and nesting depth limits as part of schema validation in a
single call rather than requiring separate enforce_limits() + validate
steps.

API additions (crates/fcp-sdk/src/lib.rs):
- validate_input_with_limits(schema, value, limits) → validates limits
  first, then schema; limit violations map to FcpError::InvalidRequest
  with INVALID_REQUEST_LIMITS_CODE
- validate_output_with_limits(schema, value, limits) → same flow but
  limit violations map to FcpError::Internal (output violations are
  internal errors, not client errors)
- validate_input() and validate_output() now delegate to the _with_limits
  variants using Limits::default(), preserving backward compatibility

Internal refactor:
- Extract validate_limits() → Result<(), LimitCheckError> as the shared
  internal limit-checking function used by both the new _with_limits
  helpers and the existing enforce_limits() public function
- Add LimitCheckError enum (Violation/Serialization) to distinguish
  limit violations from serialization failures at the internal boundary,
  allowing callers to map these to the appropriate FcpError variant
- enforce_limits() now delegates to validate_limits() and maps errors
  to FcpError, eliminating duplicated mapping logic

Re-exports (crates/fcp-sdk/src/prelude.rs):
- Add validate_input_with_limits and validate_output_with_limits to the
  SDK prelude so connectors can use them without explicit imports

Tests (crates/fcp-sdk/tests/sdk_schema_tests.rs):
- validate_input_with_limits_rejects_large_payload: verifies that an
  8-byte limit rejects a larger payload with InvalidRequest containing
  "payload size" and "exceeds limit"
- validate_output_with_limits_maps_to_internal: verifies that the same
  payload rejected as output maps to FcpError::Internal with "output
  payload exceeds limits" message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
